name: Fudge Cake with Sea Foam Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and 1 1/2 cups sugar until light.
Add a few drops of milk if needed to cream the sugar.
2. Add chocolate and blend thoroughly.
3. Sift flour with baking powder and salt into a separate bowl.
4. Add dry ingredients alternately with milk to the chocolate mixture, blending well after each addition.
5. Stir in vanilla and chopped nuts.
6. Beat 2 egg whites until frothy.
7. Gradually add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar, beating until the whites are stiff.
8. Fold the beaten egg whites into the batter.
9. Turn the batter into 2 greased and floured 9-inch layer cake pans.
10. Bake at 350°F for 30 to 40 minutes, or until the cake springs back when lightly touched with a finger in the center of the cake.
11. Cool slightly, then remove layers from pans and cool completely on a wire rack.
To make frosting:
1. Combine 3/4 cup sugar and brown sugar in a heavy saucepan.
2. Add hot water and cream of tartar and stir to blend.
3. Cover the pan and slowly bring to simmer.
4. Uncover the pan and cook until the syrup forms a long, thin thread when released from heat.
5. Remove the pan from heat.
6. Beat 3 egg whites until stiff.
7. When the syrup stops bubbling, pour it over the beaten whites in a thin stream, beating constantly until fluffy.
8. Use to frost the cooled layers.
9. If desired, melt 1 ounce semisweet chocolate with 1/4 teaspoon butter and drizzle over frosting.
10. Carefully swirl the chocolate mixture into the frosting with a thin spatula for a marbled effect.
